title: germlinevaR — read.gvr.dual() integration
text: Spine: VEP’s -severe-block pick drives row spine (one row per ALT allele), convention read.gvr(). canonical_only = TRUE (default), every kept row CANONICAL == "YES". VEP-priority shared fields: Hugo_Symbol, Consequence, IMPACT, HGVSc, HGVSp, dbSNP_RS, gnomAD/MAX_AF/ClinVar/CANONICAL/MANE/SIFT/ PolyPhen rest 80 canonical CSQ fields come VEP. FREQS (81st CSQ field added VEP --everything v113+) detected dynamically ##INFO=<ID=CSQ emitted column. Files without FREQS header get column blank, crash. + 4 LoF/NMD columns SnpEff’s INFO LOF= / NMD=: LOF_Gene, LOF_Pct_Transcripts, NMD_Gene, NMD_Pct_Transcripts. Empty ("" / NA_real_) SnpEff call LoF/NMD record. + 4 side--side comparison columns matching SnpEff ANN block: snpeff_consequence, snpeff_impact, snpeff_gene, snpeff_hgvsc. Block selection gene-aware: picks ANN block whose Gene_Name matches VEP’s Hugo_Symbol first; falls back first ANN block matching ALT allele gene-matching block exists. argument signature, defaults read.gvr() (folder / vcf_path / file / pattern / DP/GQ thresholds / genes / panel / vc_nonSyn / canonical_only / ABraOM join / writers). Tagged output: attr(maf, "annotator") == "dual". Auto-route: read.gvr(...) detects ANN+CSQ co-presence delegates, emitting: read.gvr: dual-annotated input detected (VEP CSQ + SnpEff ANN); delegating read.gvr.dual().

text: Change 1 - Global rank--row precompute (XLSX + HTML). combined drill-sheets HTML drill-tables sort per-token row pool composite key (impact_rank, -n_samples, gnomAD_AF, chromosome, start_position). baseline re-sorted pool independently per-token row indices. Since sort key depends row content (pool membership), integer rank every row global order computed inverted: Per-token sort collapses single integer-keyed order(): construction applied HTML side .html_rank_of_row (built via .gvr_rank_variants(dt) inverted), reused .dd_build_cat(). Algebraically identical per-pool sort key row-intrinsic. Change 2 - Batched XLSX combined-sheet writes. combined drill-sheet (Genes_detail, VC_detail, Clinical_detail, Impact_detail) previously written 2-3 openxlsx::writeData calls per token (blank row, label row, data block). 20 samples 50+ tokens per category, hundreds writeData calls per sheet, writeData serializes R<->C per cell dominates XLSX writer cost. new path builds entire sheet body single data.frame via data.table::rbindlist(body_parts, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE) emits ONE writeData call per sheet startRow = 2L. bold style label rows applied single vectorised addStyle() call (rep(label_rows, = n_cols) rep(seq_len(n_cols), times = length(label_rows))), mergeCells called per label row (cheap, cell-data work). Critical detail: blank/label rows must carry column types .xl_proj rbindlist preserves numeric integer types. Building NA_character_ force rbindlist upcast numerics, land XLSX shared strings (t="s") instead inline numerics (t="n"). Visible symptom: gnomADe_AF renders 7.206e-07 instead openxlsx’s 0.0000007206. fix: restores 0 sheet-content diffs baseline writer output (12/12 sheets identical small input, 31/31 big), 56 merge regions identical, styleObjects row/col positions identical. Change 3 - Drop dead filter_fn closures. cat_specs list (XLSX writer guard) .dd_token_specs list (HTML writer) carried 4 closures shape filter_fn = function(tok) ... walked dt mask rows matching one token. None closures ever invoked: .gvr_build_token_index() replaced single category-aware split() pass hands writers prebuilt list[category][token] -> integer row indices. Removing dead closures (equally dead pfx field, relic old per-token-per-sheet layout) saves 4 R closure allocations plus constant-pool entries every gvr_summary() call.

title: germlinevaR walkthrough
text: Germline variant analysis sits intersection clinical genetics bioinformatics: researchers need go annotated VCFs filtered, interpretable variant tables publication-ready figures without stitching together multiple unrelated tools. germlinevaR fills gap providing single, cohesive workflow handles full journey VCF cohort summary per-gene visualisation, built-support two widely used annotators (Ensembl VEP SnpEff) optional Brazilian population frequency annotation (ABraOM SABE-609). Inclusion Bioconductor makes package discoverable clinical genomics community ensures long-term maintenance Bioconductor release cycle.

title: germlinevaR walkthrough
text: Several Bioconductor CRAN packages address parts germline variant analysis workflow: VariantAnnotation (Bioconductor): provides low-level VCF parsing annotation infrastructure. germlinevaR builds top conceptual layer targets higher-level workflow: reads VEP/SnpEff-annotated VCFs directly, applies opinionated filtering, produces cohort summaries plots without requiring users manipulate VCF GRanges objects. maftools (Bioconductor): comprehensive somatic mutation analysis toolkit centred MAF format. germlinevaR complementary: targets germline single-sample case, produces MAF-like table compatibility, adds VEP/SnpEff multi-annotator support population-frequency filtering maftools provide. vcfR (CRAN): focuses VCF import manipulation. germlinevaR goes downstream: reading VCF applies clinical-significance population-frequency filters, generates cohort summary, renders per-gene lollipop plots. germlinevaR’s unique combination auto-routing multi-annotator reader + modular clinical filter + cohort summary + per-gene visualisation, one package external pipeline dependency.

text: read.gvr() auto-routing entry point: inspects VCF header, detects whether annotations come VEP, SnpEff, , dispatches correct sibling reader (read.gvr, read.gvr.snpeff, read.gvr.dual). read data, point read.gvr() folder containing VCF file(s) — function discovers every *.vcf.gz directory, auto-detects annotator file, returns one MAF-like row per ALT allele. files batch must share annotator; mixed VEP/SnpEff folders rejected. vignette stage shipped example temporary directory read : returned data.table carries one row per ALT allele -severe transcript, canonical 116-column schema:
code: vcf_dir <- tempfile("gvr_vig_") dir.create(vcf_dir) file.copy(     system.file("extdata", "example.vep.vcf.gz", package = "germlinevaR"),     file.path(vcf_dir, "example.vep.vcf.gz") ) #> [1] TRUE  gvr <- read.gvr(vcf_dir, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: read.gvr: ABraOM reference unreadable; 'ABraOM_AF' left blank. dim(gvr) #> [1]  62 116 head(gvr[, .(Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, IMPACT,     Tumor_Sample_Barcode)]) #>    Hugo_Symbol Variant_Classification   IMPACT Tumor_Sample_Barcode #>         <char>                 <char>   <char>               <char> #> 1:       OR4F5                 Silent      LOW            Sample_01 #> 2:       OR4F5      Missense_Mutation MODERATE            Sample_01 #> 3:       OR4F5                 Silent      LOW            Sample_01 #> 4:     Unknown                 Intron MODIFIER            Sample_01 #> 5:      WASH9P                    RNA MODIFIER            Sample_01 #> 6:      SAMD11                  5'UTR MODIFIER            Sample_01

title: germlinevaR walkthrough
text: gvr_filter() exposes population-frequency, clinical-significance, biotype, variant-classification, genotype, panel filters independent toggles. Defaults drop variants common gnomAD exomes, 1000 Genomes, ABraOM (1 %), require either pathogenic ClinVar annotation annotation : Defaults take 62-row example 7 rows, one per surviving variant classification. restrict curated gene panel, pass genes =: one BC-panel survivor — BRCA2 p.G1376A — missense rsID allele frequency catalogue, exactly profile motivates next section.
code: filt <- gvr_filter(gvr, verbose = FALSE) dim(filt) #> [1]   7 116 table(filt$Variant_Classification) #>  #>        Frame_Shift_Del        Frame_Shift_Ins                 Intron  #>                      1                      1                      1  #>      Missense_Mutation       Nonstop_Mutation            Splice_Site  #>                      1                      1                      1  #> Translation_Start_Site  #>                      1 gvr_list_panels()                    # registered panels #>  [1] "acromegaly"                           #>  [2] "breast cancer"                        #>  [3] "breast cancer somatic"                #>  [4] "familial adenomatous polyposis"       #>  [5] "gist"                                 #>  [6] "hereditary breast and ovarian cancer" #>  [7] "hereditary cancer"                    #>  [8] "hereditary colorectal cancer"         #>  [9] "hereditary gastric cancer"            #> [10] "hereditary melanoma cancer"           #> [11] "hereditary prostate cancer"           #> [12] "li-fraumeni syndrome"                 #> [13] "lynch syndrome"                       #> [14] "men1"                                 #> [15] "pheochromocytoma" panel <- gvr_panel_genes("breast cancer") panel #>  [1] "AKT1"   "ATM"    "BARD1"  "BRCA1"  "BRCA2"  "BRIP1"  "CDH1"   "CHEK2"  #>  [9] "EPCAM"  "ERBB2"  "ESR1"   "MLH1"   "MSH2"   "MSH6"   "NBN"    "NF1"    #> [17] "PALB2"  "PIK3CA" "PMS2"   "PTEN"   "RAD51C" "RAD51D" "STK11"  "TP53"  filt_bc <- gvr_filter(gvr, genes = panel, verbose = FALSE) filt_bc[, .(Hugo_Symbol, HGVSp_Short, Variant_Classification, IMPACT)] #>    Hugo_Symbol HGVSp_Short Variant_Classification   IMPACT #>         <char>      <char>                 <char>   <char> #> 1:       BRCA2    p.G1376A      Missense_Mutation MODERATE

title: germlinevaR walkthrough
text: gvr_novel() dedicated subsetter returns rows dbSNP rsID allele frequency gnomAD, 1000 Genomes, ABraOM. variants worth following : Note BRCA2 p.G1376A row panel filter also appears — single variant can satisfy several criteria .
code: novel <- gvr_novel(gvr, verbose = FALSE) dim(novel) #> [1]   3 116 novel[, .(Hugo_Symbol, HGVSp_Short, Variant_Classification, IMPACT)] #>    Hugo_Symbol HGVSp_Short Variant_Classification   IMPACT #>         <char>      <char>                 <char>   <char> #> 1:      ATAD3B                             Intron MODIFIER #> 2:    C11orf21                        Splice_Site     HIGH #> 3:       BRCA2    p.G1376A      Missense_Mutation MODERATE

title: germlinevaR walkthrough
text: gvr_summary() returns 8-section list--data.tables ready direct inspection downstream tabulation. can also write content Excel workbook, multi-page PDF, self-contained interactive HTML dashboard plotly drill-downs DT tables; disable side effects keep vignette fast: full HTML dashboard call gvr_summary(..., save_html = TRUE, out_dir = ".").

title: germlinevaR walkthrough
text: germlinevaR produces several families plots: per-gene protein-domain lollipops (gvr_lollipop), per-gene gene-structure lollipops (gvr_genepos.plot), ComplexHeatmap-based top-genes variant matrix (gvr_plot), HTML-dashboard panels gvr_summary(save_html = TRUE) embeds interactive output (also writable standalone PNGs via gvr_sum_plots()). figures rendered real cohort — tiny example MAF-like table shipped package sparse meaningful top-genes variant matrix dashboard, two families described text ; call functions MAF-like table reproduce layouts. Protein-domain lollipop — gvr_lollipop(gvr, gene = "TP53") draws variants along protein, InterPro domains fetched cached fly: Lollipop TP53 gvr_lollipop() — InterPro domains shaded Gene-structure lollipop — gvr_genepos.plot(gvr, gene = "BRCA1") draws variants along gene’s cDNA, exon/intron/UTR regions resolved via Ensembl REST local GTF: Gene-structure track BRCA1 gvr_genepos.plot() — exons/UTRs Ensembl REST Top-genes variant matrix — gvr_plot(gvr, top_n = 20, out_dir = ".") writes ComplexHeatmap top-genes variant matrix PNG top mutated genes across samples, coloured severe variant class per cell. top bar shows per-sample variant impact (HIGH / MODERATE / LOW / MODIFIER); right bar shows per-gene total burden. figure uses illustrative multi-sample data demonstrate layout: Top-genes variant matrix gvr_plot() — illustrative multi-sample data (8 samples, 20 cancer genes). real cohort, replace gvr table. gvr_sum_plots() writes panels gvr_summary(save_html = TRUE) embeds interactive dashboard (top genes, variant classification, IMPACT, top variants) standalone PNGs.

title: germlinevaR
text: self-contained toolchain single-sample germline VCFs annotated Ensembl VEP, SnpEff, — -disk VCF filtered gvr table, candidate novel variants, cohort summary, top-genes variant matrix, per-gene protein-domain lollipops, one R session.

title: germlinevaR
code: library(germlinevaR)  vcf_dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "germlinevaR") gvr     <- read.gvr(vcf_dir)             # 62 rows x 116 cols filt    <- gvr_filter(gvr)               # 7 rows (default thresholds) novel   <- gvr_novel(gvr)                # 3 candidates with no rsID / no AF summ    <- gvr_summary(filt, out_dir = tempdir())  # XLSX + PDF + HTML dashboard + 8 tables gvr_plot(filt, top_n = 20, out_dir = tempdir())    # top-genes variant matrix (PNG)

title: germlinevaR
text: germlinevaR turns one per-sample VEP- SnpEff-annotated VCFs MAF-like data.table (one row per ALT allele, one -severe transcript per allele) using three sibling readers share canonical 80-field schema auto-routed VCF header. get: tunable, modular filter (gvr_filter) population AF, clinical significance, biotype, variant classification, genotype, panel; dedicated subsetter (gvr_novel) candidate novel variants (rsID allele frequency catalogue); 8-section cohort summary (gvr_summary) optionally writes Excel workbook, multi-page PDF, self-contained interactive HTML dashboard plotly drill-downs DT tables; ComplexHeatmap-based top-genes variant matrix (gvr_plot) per-section standalone PNG/SVG/PDF exports (gvr_sum_plots); per-gene protein-domain lollipops (gvr_lollipop) --fly cached InterPro domain fetching; per-gene gene-structure (cDNA) lollipops (gvr_genepos.plot) Ensembl REST local GTF sources. Cohort folders processed parallel live heartbeat. Optional ABraOM SABE-609 allele-frequency annotation added Brazilian cohorts.

what: Generate a Vector of Colors From a Named Palette — gvr_color_palette
title: Generate a Vector of Colors From a Named Palette — gvr_color_palette
text: gvr_color_palette() returns character vector n hex codes drawn one 52 named palettes covering ColorBrewer, viridis, scientific journals, publishers, corporate brands, colorblind-friendly schemes, gradients, classic R palettes.  helper gvr_lollipop() uses internally resolve variant_palette domain_palette arguments, also exported users can build color vectors plot.

title: Generate a Vector of Colors From a Named Palette — gvr_color_palette
code: gvr_color_palette(palette = "okabe_ito", n = 8L)

title: Generate a Vector of Colors From a Named Palette — gvr_color_palette
text: palette Character string. Name palette use.  See gvr_list_palettes() full catalog 52 names.  Defaults "okabe_ito", colorblind-friendly palette recommended Okabe & Ito (2008). n Integer.  Number colors return.  n exceeds base length discrete palette, intermediate colors interpolated via grDevices::colorRampPalette().

title: Generate a Vector of Colors From a Named Palette — gvr_color_palette
text: character vector length n containing hex color codes.

title: Generate a Vector of Colors From a Named Palette — gvr_color_palette
text: 52 palettes grouped following families.  Names identical exposed sibling plot_multiple_compounds() function two packages share color vocabulary. Base: "hue", "ggplot2", "default"  (ggplot2 default). ColorBrewer qualitative (good distinct categories): "set1", "set2", "set3", "dark2", "paired", "accent", "pastel1", "pastel2". ColorBrewer sequential (ordered data): "blues", "reds", "greens", "purples", "oranges", "greys". ColorBrewer diverging: "spectral", "rdylbu", "rdylgn", "piyg", "prgn". Viridis (perceptually uniform): "viridis", "magma", "inferno", "plasma". Journal palettes: "nature", "science", "cell", "plos", "elife". Publisher palettes: "bmc", "frontiers", "wiley", "elsevier", "oxford", "springer", "acs", "rsc". Corporate brand palettes: "ibm", "google", "microsoft", "twitter". Colorblind-friendly: "okabe_ito", "colorblind", "cud", "tol". Gradient palettes: "blue_red", "green_red", "purple_orange", "cool_warm", "blue_yellow". Classic R: "rainbow", "heat", "terrain", "topo", "cm". Optional dependencies: ColorBrewer palettes use RColorBrewer available fall back interpolated base colors otherwise.  Viridis palettes use viridisLite available fall back manual hex sequences otherwise.  packages Suggests:.

what: Clear the auto-fetched protein-domain cache used by gvr_lollipop — gvr_domain_cache_clear
title: Clear the auto-fetched protein-domain cache used by gvr_lollipop — gvr_domain_cache_clear
text: Deletes cached InterPro domain .rds files written gvr_lollipop(..., domains = "auto"). Useful InterPro releases new version (roughly quarterly) want fresh annotations next call. Also clears matching -memory session cache next call actually re-fetches instead returning -memory copy.

title: Clear the auto-fetched protein-domain cache used by gvr_lollipop — gvr_domain_cache_clear
code: gvr_domain_cache_clear(   gene = NULL,   organism = NULL,   cache_dir = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Clear the auto-fetched protein-domain cache used by gvr_lollipop — gvr_domain_cache_clear
text: gene Character(1) NULL. Gene symbol delete cache. NULL (default) deletes every cached domain file cache directory (.e., every file matching ^domains_interpro_.*\\.rds$). organism Integer character NULL. NCBI taxonomy id used part cache key. gene given organism NULL, every taxonomy variant gene deleted (.e., pattern ^domains_interpro_<GENE>_.*\\.rds$). Default NULL. cache_dir Character(1) NULL. Override cache directory. NULL (default) triggers precedence chain . verbose Logical(1). Print one line per deleted file. Default TRUE.

title: Clear the auto-fetched protein-domain cache used by gvr_lollipop — gvr_domain_cache_clear
text: Invisibly, character vector file paths deleted (length 0 none).

title: Clear the auto-fetched protein-domain cache used by gvr_lollipop — gvr_domain_cache_clear
text: cache directory resolved precedence chain used gvr_lollipop(), chain finds existing directory; never creates one. precedence (first hit wins) : cache_dir argument (explicit override). Environment variable GVR_CACHE_DIR. R option getOption("germlinevaR.cache_dir"). tools::R_user_dir("germlinevaR", "cache"). file.path(tempdir(), "germlinevaR_cache"). resolved directory exist, helper returns invisibly verbose message files deleted (error; missing cache directory simply means nothing clear).

title: Clear the auto-fetched protein-domain cache used by gvr_lollipop — gvr_domain_cache_clear
code: ## Clear the cache for a specific gene (safe to run; no-op if cache is empty) gvr_domain_cache_clear(gene = "TP53") #> gvr_domain_cache_clear: no cache directory found (nothing to clear).  ## Clear everything (all genes, all organisms) gvr_domain_cache_clear() #> gvr_domain_cache_clear: no cache directory found (nothing to clear).  ## Clear only TP53 across all organisms gvr_domain_cache_clear(gene = "TP53") #> gvr_domain_cache_clear: no cache directory found (nothing to clear).  ## Clear only human TP53 gvr_domain_cache_clear(gene = "TP53", organism = 9606) #> gvr_domain_cache_clear: no cache directory found (nothing to clear).

what: Modular, individually-toggleable filtering of a read.gvr table — gvr_filter
title: Modular, individually-toggleable filtering of a read.gvr table — gvr_filter
text: Applies set independent variant filters MAF-like table produced read.gvr(). distinct filter argument; setting argument NULL disables filter entirely (rows removed ). defaults, gvr_filter(gvr) reproduces canonical rare / clinically-relevant / called-genotype pipeline (AF filters + CLIN_SIG + GT exclusion).

title: Modular, individually-toggleable filtering of a read.gvr table — gvr_filter
code: gvr_filter(   gvr,   gnomADe_AF = 0.01,   AF = 0.01,   ABraOM_AF = 0.01,   gnomADe_AF_keep_missing = TRUE,   AF_keep_missing = TRUE,   ABraOM_AF_keep_missing = TRUE,   clin_sig_terms = c("likely_pathogenic", "pathogenic", "uncertain_significance"),   clin_sig_keep_missing = TRUE,   remove_benign = FALSE,   biotype_keep = NULL,   gt_exclude = c("0", "0/0"),   vc_nonSyn = FALSE,   missense_only = FALSE,   genes = NULL,   panel = NULL,   hpo = NULL,   save_excel = FALSE,   out_dir = NULL,   file_prefix = "gvr_filter",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Modular, individually-toggleable filtering of a read.gvr table — gvr_filter
text: gvr data.table / data.frame read.gvr() (compatible). Filtered copy; input object modified. gnomADe_AF Numeric upper threshold gnomAD exome AF column gnomADe_AF (keep rows AF < threshold). NULL disables filter. Default 0.01. AF Numeric upper threshold AF column (gnomAD genome / VCF allele frequency). NULL disables filter. Default 0.01. ABraOM_AF Numeric upper threshold Brazilian-cohort (ABraOM SABE 609) column ABraOM_AF. NULL disables filter. Default 0.01. gnomADe_AF_keep_missing Logical; TRUE (default), keep rows whose gnomADe_AF missing (NA ""); FALSE drop . Ignored gnomADe_AF NULL. AF_keep_missing Logical; missing-value handling AF filter. TRUE (default) keeps missing. Ignored AF NULL. ABraOM_AF_keep_missing Logical; missing-value handling ABraOM filter. TRUE (default) retains variants absent Brazilian cohort (absence often means "catalogued", "common"). Ignored ABraOM_AF NULL. clin_sig_terms Character vector clinical-significance terms keep (substring, case-insensitive, -combined). NULL disables CLIN_SIG filter. Default: c("likely_pathogenic","pathogenic","uncertain_significance"). clin_sig_keep_missing Logical; TRUE (default) rows missing CLIN_SIG (NA/"") kept. relevant clin_sig_terms non-NULL. remove_benign Logical; TRUE, remove rows whose CLIN_SIG contains "benign" (substring, case-insensitive). catches benign, likely_benign, compound annotations like "uncertain_significance&likely_benign". Applied clin_sig_terms keep-filter, row matched wanted term also contains "benign" still removed. FALSE (default) remove benign rows. biotype_keep Character vector BIOTYPE values keep (exact match via %%). NULL (default) disables biotype filter — biotypes kept. Pass e.g. c("protein_coding", "protein_coding_LoF") restrict protein-coding transcripts. gt_exclude Character vector GT values remove (exact match). NULL disables genotype filter. Default: c("0","0/0"). vc_nonSyn Logical character vector. Controls Variant_Classification values retained. FALSE (default) keeps . TRUE keeps 9 protein-altering classes (Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site, Nonsense_Mutation, Nonstop_Mutation, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Missense_Mutation). custom character vector keeps classifications. Rows missing/blank Variant_Classification removed filter active. missense_only Logical; TRUE, keep rows whose Variant_Classification equals "Missense_Mutation" (added vN+5). Default FALSE preserves prior behaviour byte--byte. Combines non-contradictorily vc_nonSyn: vc_nonSyn runs first (keeping 9 protein-altering classes), missense_only narrows missense subset. Errors clear message Variant_Classification missing. genes Character vector Hugo_Symbols keep (exact, case-insensitive), NULL (default) keep genes. panel Character vector curated disease panel name(s) (e.g. "breast cancer", "hereditary prostate cancer", "gist"). Resolved gene vectors via gvr_panel_genes() unioned genes (dedup, uppercased) "Gene subset" filter runs. See gvr_list_panels() full registry. NULL (default) disables panel filtering; behaviour byte-identical omitting argument. hpo Character vector Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO) term id(s), e.g. "HP:0003002" (Breast carcinoma). Resolved gene vectors via gvr_hpo_genes() unioned genes genes / panel "Gene subset" filter runs. Lenient input accepted: "HP:0003002", "hp:0003002", "3002", "0003002" normalise canonical "HP:0003002". exact-term associations used (ontology-descendant expansion). HPO table downloaded cached tools::R_user_dir("germlinevaR", "cache"); see gvr_hpo_genes() offline / air-gapped use via hpo_path=. NULL (default) disables HPO filtering; behaviour byte-identical omitting argument. save_excel Logical; TRUE, also write FILTERED table .xlsx workbook (single "Filtered" sheet) <out_dir>/<file_prefix>.xlsx. Requires openxlsx package (Suggests dependency); installed export skipped warning. Default FALSE. write side effect : returned data.table identical whether save_excel TRUE. out_dir Output directory Excel file. NULL (default) uses current working directory. Created exist. used save_excel = TRUE. file_prefix Filename prefix (without extension) Excel file. Default "gvr_filter" -> gvr_filter.xlsx. used save_excel = TRUE. verbose Logical; TRUE (default) print per-filter breakdown (rows -> rows removed active step).

title: Modular, individually-toggleable filtering of a read.gvr table — gvr_filter
text: data.table surviving rows, columns input. plain data.frame input returned data.table. input object modified. verbose = TRUE, per-filter breakdown (rows -> , rows removed active step) printed runs.

title: Modular, individually-toggleable filtering of a read.gvr table — gvr_filter
text: Filters applied fixed order; step operates survivors previous one: gnomAD exome AF        - gnomADe_AF        (+ gnomADe_AF_keep_missing) gnomAD genome / VCF AF - AF                (+ AF_keep_missing) ABraOM AF             - ABraOM_AF         (+ ABraOM_AF_keep_missing) Clinical significance  - clin_sig_terms    (+ clin_sig_keep_missing) Remove benign          - remove_benign Biotype                - biotype_keep Genotype exclusion     - gt_exclude Variant classification - vc_nonSyn Gene subset            - genes (unioned panel hpo) Important data notes (true read.gvr() output): AF columns CHARACTER (e.g. "0.8781"), coerced .numeric() comparison. "Missing" means EITHER NA empty string "" (read.gvr() uses "" absent values). treated missing everywhere function. CLIN_SIG matching SUBSTRING + case-insensitive. compound annotation "pathogenic&benign" "uncertain_significance&likely_benign&benign" KEPT CONTAINS wanted term. matches dplyr str_detect() / grepl() convention. Use exact-token matching split CLIN_SIG . default gt_exclude = c("0", "0/0") -op data whose GT column contains called alt genotypes (e.g. 0/1, 1/1, 1/2); retained portability data carry "0"/"0/0". input never modified: filtering operates internal data.table copy.

what: Gene-track lollipop plot on a cDNA axis — gvr_genepos.plot
title: Gene-track lollipop plot on a cDNA axis — gvr_genepos.plot
text: Draws per-gene track plot exon, intron, UTR segments one transcript, overlaid lollipops placed cDNA-position x-axis using HGVSc field table row. Colours follow Variant_Classification using palette set gvr_lollipop().

title: Gene-track lollipop plot on a cDNA axis — gvr_genepos.plot
code: gvr_genepos.plot(   gvr,   gene,   transcript_id = NULL,   vc_keep = NULL,   color_by = c("vc", "region"),   intron_scale = c("fixed", "proportional", "log"),   intron_visual_bp = 200L,   utr_visual_bp = NULL,   label_top = 5L,   hotspot_window = 20L,   hotspot_min_n = 4,   stem_alpha = 0.6,   point_size = 3,   bar_color = "grey85",   bar_border = "grey40",   utr_color = "grey70",   intron_color = "grey60",   exon_color = "steelblue",   variant_palette = "gvr",   base_size = 12,   axis_text_size = 11,   axis_title_size = 12,   ensembl_release = NULL,   gtf_path = NULL,   cache_dir = NULL,   out_dir = ".",   out_subdir = "gvr_genepos",   out_prefix = NULL,   format = c("png", "svg", "pdf", "tiff"),   width = 10,   height = 4,   dpi = 300,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Gene-track lollipop plot on a cDNA axis — gvr_genepos.plot
text: gvr MAF-like data.table produced read.gvr(). Required columns: Hugo_Symbol, Transcript_ID, HGVSc, Variant_Classification, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, NCBI_Build. MANE_SELECT CANONICAL used transcript_id auto-resolved. gene Character(1). HGNC symbol (matches Hugo_Symbol). transcript_id Character(1) NULL. Ensembl stable id (ENST...). NULL triggers auto-resolution described . vc_keep Character vector NULL. non-NULL, variants whose Variant_Classification vc_keep kept. Otherwise default filter gvr_lollipop() applied (non-synonymous plus splice plus UTR). color_by Character(1). One "vc" (colour Variant_Classification; default) "region" (colour track region: cds / utr5 / utr3 / splice / intron). Currently "vc" stable visual contract. intron_scale Character(1). One "fixed" (default; constant visual width per intron, intron_visual_bp pixels), "proportional" (true bp), "log" (log10-scaled). intron_visual_bp Integer(1). Visual width every intron intron_scale == "fixed". Default 200. utr_visual_bp Integer(1) NULL. non-NULL, total UTR visual length rescaled many bp regardless real UTR length (useful long UTRs). NULL (default) means real length. label_top Integer(1). Number top-counted cDNA positions label (matches gvr_lollipop()). hotspot_window Numeric(1). Sliding-window width (bp cDNA axis) hotspot detection. Default 20. hotspot_min_n Numeric(1). Minimum distinct cDNA positions inside window drawn hotspot band. Default 4. Pass Inf disable. stem_alpha Numeric(1). Lollipop stem opacity. Default 0.6. point_size Numeric(1). Dot size. Default 3. bar_color, bar_border Character(1). Fill / border colour protein-body bar drawn coding exons. Default "grey85" / "grey40". utr_color, intron_color, exon_color Character(1). Track region fill colours. Default "grey70" / "grey60" / "steelblue". variant_palette See gvr_lollipop() full grammar ("gvr", palette name, named override vector). base_size, axis_text_size, axis_title_size Numeric. Text sizes. ensembl_release Integer NULL. Future use; currently unused (REST latest always queried). gtf_path Character(1) NULL. Offline GTF override. non-NULL, REST called. cache_dir Character(1), NULL, FALSE. Cache directory. FALSE disables -disk caching. out_dir Character(1) NULL. Output directory root. out_subdir Character(1). Subfolder out_dir. Default "gvr_genepos". out_prefix Character(1) NULL. File prefix; default paste(gene, transcript_id, sep = "_"). format Character(1). One "png", "svg", "pdf", "tiff". Default "png". width, height, dpi Numeric. Plot size resolution. verbose Logical(1). TRUE, emit progress messages.

title: Resolve HPO phenotype terms to associated genes — gvr_hpo_genes
text: Retrieves genes associated one Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO) terms using HPO phenotype--gene association table. mainly used read.gvr() gvr_filter() hpo argument, can also called directly inspect gene set filtering. HPO table downloaded cached tools::R_user_dir("germlinevaR", "cache"). Cached files older max_age_days days trigger automatic re-download; setting refresh_cache = TRUE forces refresh regardless age. Use hpo_path = <local file> offline / air-gapped operation. Input HPO identifiers lenient-parsed: "HP:0003002", "hp:0003002", "3002", "0003002" accepted normalised canonical "HP:0003002" lookup. normalisation happens message() emitted typos remain visible. ontology-descendant expansion performed release: genes associated exact HPO terms supplied returned. include descendants, resolve first (e.g. via ontologyIndex::get_descendants()).

title: Per-gene amino-acid lollipop plot for a germline gvr table — gvr_lollipop
text: Builds ggplot2 lollipop plot every protein-altering variant single gene read.gvr() / gvr_filter() / gvr_novel() table. amino-acid position stem; sample-variant carried position one dot stacked stem; dots coloured Variant_Classification using package's colourblind-safe palette. horizontal protein-body bar drawn along x-axis (lollipop-style); protein domains overlaid coloured rectangles top bar. default, domains fetched automatically EBI InterPro REST API (domains = "auto"). Pass domains = NULL plain bar domains, supply data.frame custom domain coordinates. default writes .svg .png disk (matching gvr_plot() behaviour).

code: gvr_lollipop(   gvr,   gene,   vc_keep = NULL,   protein_length = NULL,   protein_length_strict = FALSE,   domains = "auto",   organism = 9606L,   cache_dir = NULL,   label_top = 5L,   domain_label_min_frac = 0.05,   domain_label_mode = c("name", "id", "number", "none", "auto"),   domain_name_abbrev = TRUE,   domain_label_position = c("inside", "below"),   domain_label_wrap = TRUE,   hotspot_window = 20L,   hotspot_min_n = 4,   stem_alpha = 0.6,   point_size = 3,   stem_color = "grey50",   bar_color = "grey85",   bar_border = "grey40",   base_size = 12,   axis_text_size = 11,   axis_title_size = 12,   axis_text_color = "grey20",   axis_title_color = "black",   axis_line_color = "grey40",   axis_line_width = 0.4,   out_dir = ".",   out_subdir = "gvr_lollipop",   out_prefix = gene,   width = 10,   height = 4,   dpi = 300,   variant_palette = "gvr",   domain_palette = "okabe_ito",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Per-gene amino-acid lollipop plot for a germline gvr table — gvr_lollipop
text: gvr MAF-like data.table / data.frame read.gvr(), compatible table least Hugo_Symbol, HGVSp_Short, Variant_Classification, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Protein_position. gene Character(1). gene symbol (matched Hugo_Symbol). vc_keep Character vector Variant_Classification values keep. NULL (default) uses 9-class protein-altering set gvr_filter(). protein_length Integer(1) NULL. Sets x-axis length (amino acids). Resolved following precedence: UniProt canonical sequence length, fetched via UniProt REST query used domains = "auto". Wins default; see protein_length_strict opt . User-supplied value protein_length_strict = TRUE, UniProt unreachable. Mode <pos>/<total> totals parsed table Protein_position column (entries without / skipped). ceiling(max(positions) * 1.1) fallback. final value always least max(positions) mutation clipped. one-line message() emitted UniProt overrides user-supplied value 5%. protein_length_strict Logical(1). TRUE, user's protein_length kept verbatim UniProt canonical length used (max-position safety net still applies). Default FALSE. domains "auto", NULL, data.frame. Default "auto": domains fetched EBI InterPro REST API cached. NULL draws plain bar domain rectangles. data.frame columns start, end (optional name, color) draws custom domain rectangles. See Details three accepted forms. organism Integer string. NCBI taxonomy id used domains = "auto" (passed UniProt search organism_id). Default 9606L (human). Ignored domains "auto". cache_dir NULL, directory path, FALSE. Controls -disk cache used domains = "auto". NULL (default) triggers precedence chain documented Details. FALSE disables -disk caching. Ignored domains "auto". label_top Integer(1). Number top-counted positions label. 0 disables labels, Inf labels every position. Default 5L. domain_label_min_frac Numeric(1) [0, 1]. Minimum domain width fraction protein_length domain label rendered "name" "id" modes. Default 0.05 (5%). Lower long proteins (e.g. 0.01 even small domains get labelled). Ignored domain_label_mode = "number" (domains labelled) "none". domain_label_mode One "name" (default), "id", "number", "none". Controls domain rectangles labelled. "name": human-readable InterPro name, repelled bar leader lines (uses ggrepel::geom_text_repel() available). "id": InterPro accession (e.g. IPR011615); falls back name accession missing (user-supplied data.frame without ). "number": numbers 1..N centered rectangle, companion "Domains" legend mapping number full name. Requires optional ggnewscale package; without , numbers render. "auto": opt-heuristic. Use "number" protein_length > 2000 aa >= 5 domains drawn (long, densely-annotated proteins -plot names overlap), otherwise use "name". verbose = TRUE, resolved mode reason printed. "none": labels (rely variant legend ). domain_name_abbrev Logical(1). TRUE (default), apply small set substitutions compress verbose InterPro domain names (e.g. "von Willebrand factor, type D domain" becomes "VWF type-D") labels fit inside narrower rectangles domain_label_mode = "name". Set FALSE keep raw InterPro names verbatim. domain_label_position One "inside" (default) "". Controls InterPro domain labels render relative domain rectangle. "inside" places labels centred inside rectangle automatically chosen black/white text colour contrast domain fill (WCAG luminance rule). "" reproduces legacy layout used earlier versions (labels bar, leader lines rectangle). domain_label_position = "inside", labels fit inside domain rectangle (estimated character count vs rectangle width) fall back "" style leader line per-domain basis information lost. verbose message reports count overflowing labels. domain_label_wrap Logical(1). TRUE (default), inside-mode domain labels fit one line wrapped onto second line nearest comma (space commas) font shrunk 10% steps floor 60% base. Wrapping per-domain: labels already fit one line stay unchanged. label still overflows 2-line + 60% font cascade, falls back existing -bar leader (information lost). Set FALSE keep pre-Phase-L behaviour: 1-line labels , overflow going directly -bar fallback. hotspot_window Integer(1). Sliding-window width (amino acids) used detect mutation hotspots. hotspot region containing least hotspot_min_n distinct variant positions within hotspot_window aa. Drawn soft translucent vertical band behind bar/domains/stems. Default 20L (publication-tight rule). Increase noisier/exploratory cohorts. hotspot_min_n Numeric(1). Minimum distinct variant positions inside hotspot_window-wide region drawn hotspot band. Default 4. Pass Inf disable hotspot detection entirely. Counting uses unique aa positions (sample counts), single recurrently-hit position create band. stem_alpha Numeric(1) [0, 1]. Opacity lollipop stems. Default 0.6. Lower dense plots; raise 1 original solid stems. point_size Numeric(1). Dot size. Default 3. stem_color Character(1). Stem (vertical line) colour. Default "grey50". bar_color Character(1). Protein-body bar fill colour. Default "grey85". bar_border Character(1) NA. Protein-body bar border colour. Default "grey40". base_size Numeric(1). ggplot2 base font size. Default 12. axis_text_size Numeric(1). Font size axis tick labels (aa positions x, count y). Default 11, matching existing visual output. Set 12 higher publication-style figures. axis_title_size Numeric(1). Font size axis titles ("Amino acid position" / "Variant count"). Default 12. Set 14 sibling- package publication defaults. axis_text_color Character(1). Colour axis tick labels. Default "grey20" (current visual). Set "black" sibling-package publication defaults. axis_title_color Character(1). Colour axis titles. Default "black". axis_line_color Character(1). Colour axis lines ticks. Default "grey40" (current visual). Set "black" sibling- package publication defaults. axis_line_width Numeric(1). Line width (ggplot2 linewidth units) axis lines ticks. Default 0.4 (current visual). Set 0.8 sibling-package publication defaults. out_dir Character(1) NULL. Output directory root SVG/PNG files. Created exist. Default "." (current working directory), matching gvr_plot(). Set NULL suppress file output. default gvr_lollipop/ subfolder created inside out_dir (see out_subdir). out_subdir Character(1) NULL. Subfolder out_dir collect lollipop outputs. Default "gvr_lollipop", matching gvr_genepos.plot() / gvr_summary() convention. Set NULL ("") write files directly out_dir subfolder. out_prefix Character(1) NULL. Filename prefix; output files <out_prefix>_lollipop.svg / <out_prefix>_lollipop.png. Default gene (gene symbol), default out_dir = "." out_subdir = "gvr_lollipop", gvr_lollipop(f, "TP53") writes ./gvr_lollipop/TP53_lollipop.svg ./gvr_lollipop/TP53_lollipop.png. Set NULL suppress file output. width Numeric(1). Plot width inches. Default 10. height Numeric(1). Plot height inches. Default 4. dpi Numeric(1). PNG resolution. Default 300. variant_palette Character. Variant colour assignment. Accepts three forms. (1) palette name (e.g. "gvr", "nature", "set2"); default "gvr" semantic palette , e.g., Missense_Mutation always green Nonsense_Mutation always orange regardless classes present. palette name treated positional ordinal (colours assigned order classes appear data). (2) named character vector explicit hex codes specific Variant_Classification values, e.g. c(Missense_Mutation = "#FF0000", Nonsense_Mutation = "#000000"); classes listed inherit "gvr" defaults. (3) mixed vector one unnamed element used fallback palette name, e.g. c(Missense_Mutation = "#FF00FF", "nature") (Missense magenta, classes nature palette). Use gvr_list_palettes() discover names gvr_color_palette() inspect colours. domain_palette Character. Palette used InterPro domain rectangle fills. Accepts three forms variant_palette: palette name (default "okabe_ito"), named vector keyed accession domain name, mixed vector fallback palette name. Unlike variant_palette "gvr" semantic special case; default cycles 9-colour colour-blind-safe palette across domains. verbose Logical(1). TRUE (default), emit progress messages (counts, dropped rows, file paths, cache hits, network calls).

title: Per-gene amino-acid lollipop plot for a germline gvr table — gvr_lollipop
text: ggplot2 object. default SVG PNG files also written disk side effect. Set out_dir = NULL suppress file output.

title: Per-gene amino-acid lollipop plot for a germline gvr table — gvr_lollipop
text: Variant classes filtered canonical protein-altering set (9 classes used gvr_filter() vc_nonSyn = TRUE): Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site, Nonsense_Mutation, Nonstop_Mutation, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Missense_Mutation. default can overridden vc_keep argument. Amino-acid positions extracted HGVSp_Short using regex ^p\\.[-Z*]([0-9]+). Rows whose HGVSp_Short match (complex indels p.M1?, splice variants without p., etc.) dropped warning showing many rows dropped. Multi-amino-acid descriptors (e.g. p.D1850_E1852del) match first amino-acid number, standard 5'-position convention. Protein length (x-axis upper bound) derived Protein_position column: value string "<current>/<total>". -common total across gene's rows used. row parseable total, x-axis falls back max(position) * 1.1. protein_length argument overrides . Stacking: surviving sample-variant becomes one dot. Dots sharing amino-acid position stacked vertically, height stack equals count sample-variants position. Stems run top protein-body bar top stack. Labels: top-label_top positions count annotated HGVSp_Short (e.g. p.R175H). multiple distinct HGVSp_Short share position, -common one shown followed (+N ). Set label_top = 0 disable labels, label_top = Inf label every position. ggrepel installed, labels placed via ggrepel::geom_text_repel(); otherwise plain geom_text() used. Protein-body bar: thin horizontal rectangle drawn straddling y = 0 x = 0 x = protein_length. Bar height scales visible stack height (4 percent y range, clamped [0.15, 0.5]). y-axis expanded slightly zero accommodate bar. Domains: domains accepts three forms: "auto" (default) - function calls EBI InterPro REST API gene (gene -> UniProt accession via rest.uniprot.org, UniProt -> InterPro domains via www.ebi.ac.uk/interpro), keeps InterPro-integrated entries (source_database == "interpro", e.g. IPR011615), caches result, converts data.frame shape . Requires optional packages httr jsonlite; either missing, HTTP / parsing step fails, warning emitted plot still renders plain bar. NULL - domain rectangles, just plain bar. data.frame required columns start, end optional columns name (label, shown rectangle wide enough) color (hex code R colour name; auto-assigned colourblind-safe palette missing). Rows entirely outside [0, protein_length] dropped; partial overlaps clipped. Cache (used domains = "auto", default): resolved cache directory picked first writable entry precedence chain: cache_dir argument (explicit override; pass FALSE disable -disk caching entirely). Environment variable GVR_CACHE_DIR (useful HPC /scratch/$USER). R option getOption("germlinevaR.cache_dir") (.Rprofile). tools::R_user_dir("germlinevaR", "cache") (XDG-compliant default). file.path(tempdir(), "germlinevaR_cache") (last-resort, per-session). Cached files: <cache_dir>/domains_interpro_<GENE>_<ORG>.rds. force refresh, delete file (use gvr_domain_cache_clear() helper). Empty-gene behaviour: row matches gene, none survives vc_keep filter, none parseable amino-acid position, function issues warning returns ggplot2 object single centered "protein-altering variants " annotation. files written empty-gene plots. CLIN_SIG split & /, variant annotated "pathogenic&benign" increments categories; category counts therefore sum \(\ge\) number variants. missing/unclassified row counts NA/"" CLIN_SIG. top_genes_per_sample - named list data.tables, one per sample, containing top_n_genes genes variants sample (columns: Hugo_Symbol, <sample_name>). Unknown/blank genes excluded. top_variants - top_n_variants frequent variants dbSNP_RS (rsID), per-sample counts + Total. Rows blank/missing dbSNP_RS excluded. dbSNP_RS column absent, section skipped warning. impact - counts per VEP IMPACT (HIGH/MODERATE/LOW/MODIFIER), severity order rather count order. section tables core return value. default function also writes Excel workbook (save_excel = TRUE) PDF report (save_pdf = TRUE) out_dir/gvr_summary/; set either FALSE skip . cohort top-genes variant matrix lives gvr_plot(). Data conventions: "Missing" means NA empty string "". Unknown/blank gene symbols Hugo_Symbol c(".", "", "Unknown"); excluded distinct-gene tally top_genes, variants still counted totals (reported "variants gene symbol"). title: Convert dual-annotated germline VCF(s) (VEP + SnpEff) to an MAF-like data.table — read.gvr.dual
text: Field-level priority: VEP wins. canonical table columns 80 (81 FREQS) VEP-style CSQ columns hold VEP's pick exclusively. SnpEff data added additional columns: snpeff_consequence, snpeff_impact, snpeff_gene, snpeff_hgvsc – SnpEff ANN block matching ALT allele gene VEP's pick (-deleterious block multiple match). Empty SnpEff ANN block allele. LOF_Gene, LOF_Pct_Transcripts – SnpEff's loss--function prediction (gene flagged, fraction transcripts affected) INFO LOF= field. Empty LoF call. NMD_Gene, NMD_Pct_Transcripts – SnpEff's nonsense-mediated- decay prediction INFO NMD= field. Empty NMD call. Transcript pick: VEP drives row spine (one row per ALT allele). VEP run --per_gene (one transcript per gene per allele), chosen transcript VEP's -severe across genes allele; otherwise standard VEP -severe-block ranking applies. SnpEff annotations attached row ANN block matching allele (preferentially) Gene_Name. Schema additions vs read.gvr() (VEP-): FREQS – 81st canonical CSQ field, populated VEP run --everything (v113+). Names gnomAD population MAX_AF observed (e.g. "gnomADg_ASJ"). Empty CSQ header include field. 4 LOF/NMD columns () 4 snpeff_* parallel columns () Total: ~124 columns (vs ~116 read.gvr() read.gvr.snpeff()). behaviour, defaults, arguments, filtering options identical read.gvr(); see function's documentation full option reference.

title: Convert dual-annotated germline VCF(s) (VEP + SnpEff) to an MAF-like data.table — read.gvr.dual
code: ## The function signature is exported and callable: is.function(read.gvr.dual) #> [1] TRUE  # \donttest{ ## read.gvr.dual() expects VCFs annotated with BOTH VEP (CSQ INFO field) ## AND SnpEff (ANN INFO field) in the same record. The shipped example ## VCF is VEP-only, so a real dual-annotated example needs your own ## VCFs; we therefore guard the call on the directory existing, so the ## example skips cleanly on machines without the data. dual_dir <- "/path/to/dual-annotated-vcfs/" if (dir.exists(dual_dir)) {     gvr <- read.gvr.dual(folder = dual_dir)      ## Or via the auto-router in read.gvr() when the VCF header declares     ## both VEP and SnpEff INFO fields:     gvr <- read.gvr(dual_dir)      ## Compare VEP vs SnpEff picks on high-impact variants:     gvr[IMPACT == "HIGH" & snpeff_impact != "" & IMPACT != snpeff_impact,         .(Hugo_Symbol, Consequence, IMPACT, snpeff_gene, snpeff_consequence,             snpeff_impact)] } # }

text: MAF-like data.table: one row per variant allele, MAF-like core columns, VEP CSQ fields, key GATK QC fields, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, (enabled) Genotype ABraOM_AF columns. TSV/RDS files written side effect write_tsv/write_rds TRUE.

title: Convert VEP-annotated germline VCF(s) to an MAF-like data.table — read.gvr
text: Output behaviour: Returns final MAF-like data.table, one row per variant ALLELE (multi-allelic sites split). single -severe transcript chosen per allele (VEP severity -> CANONICAL -> MANE_SELECT -> transcript id). Columns include MAF-like core fields, VEP CSQ fields (read VCF header), key GATK QC fields. FILTER retained column variants (PASS non-PASS) kept. Tumor_Seq_Allele1/Tumor_Seq_Allele2 zygosity-aware (vcf2maf-style), optional Genotype column (Tumor_Seq_Allele1/Tumor_Seq_Allele2, e.g. "T/C") added next alleles. variant keeps source sample Tumor_Sample_Barcode. Absent values written empty string "" (NA); downstream gvr_filter() / gvr_summary() treat NA "" identically "missing". Processing options: MULTI-FILE: folder mode, every file matching pattern (default "*_NN.vcf.gz") converted row-bound. HGVS CLEANUP (strip_hgvs_prefix): strips Ensembl feature prefix HGVSc/HGVSp (e.g. "ENST00000831140.1:n.1889G>" -> "n.1889G>"). DEDUP (dedup_columns): removes duplicate-named columns values byte--byte identical across rows (otherwise keeps + warns). ABraOM (add_abraom): joins Brazilian ABraOM SABE-609 allele frequency ABraOM_AF column (downloaded/cached abraom_url). GENOTYPE-QUALITY FILTER (min_DP/min_GQ): keeps record iff DP > min_DP GQ > min_GQ; mirrors bcftools view -e 'FORMAT/DP<=X | FORMAT/GQ<=Y'. Set either NULL disable field; set NULL disable genotype filter entirely. GENE SUBSET (genes): restrict set Hugo_Symbols (exact, case-insensitive).

code: ## read.gvr() reads VEP/SnpEff-annotated VCFs and returns a parsed ## data.table. Reading the bundled 62-variant fixture takes ~20s on ## a typical CI worker, so the real read.gvr() calls are wrapped in ## a donttest block below; a pre-parsed equivalent .rds is also bundled.  ## The function is exported and callable: is.function(read.gvr) #> [1] TRUE  ## Pre-parsed equivalent (instantaneous; same shape and content as the ## result of read.gvr() on the same fixture, minus the ABraOM_AF column ## which was added in a later code revision): gvr <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "example_gvr.rds",     package = "germlinevaR")) dim(gvr) #> [1]  62 115  # \donttest{ ## Real read.gvr() call on the bundled VCF directory: vcf_dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "germlinevaR") gvr <- read.gvr(vcf_dir, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: read.gvr: ABraOM reference unreadable; 'ABraOM_AF' left blank. dim(gvr)            # 62 rows x 116 columns #> [1]  62 116  ## Same call but with write-out to tempdir out <- tempdir() gvr2 <- read.gvr(vcf_dir, write_tsv = TRUE, write_rds = TRUE,     out_dir = out, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: read.gvr: ABraOM reference unreadable; 'ABraOM_AF' left blank. list.files(out, pattern = "\\.(tsv|rds)$") #> [1] "example.vep.gvr.tsv.rds" "example.vep.gvr.tsv.tsv"  ## Single-file mode: full path to the bundled VCF vcf_file <- system.file("extdata", "example.vep.vcf.gz",     package = "germlinevaR") gvr3 <- read.gvr(vcf_path = vcf_file, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: read.gvr: ABraOM reference unreadable; 'ABraOM_AF' left blank. nrow(gvr3) #> [1] 62 # }

Lenient input forms accepted normalised: "HP:0003002", "hp:0003002", "hp:3002", "3002", "0003002" coerce canonical "HP:0003002". exact terms used; descendants ontology expanded. annotation file fetched per session cached tools::R_user_dir() "germlinevaR" "cache"; cache auto-refreshed 30 days. offline / hermetic runs, point options(gvr.hpo_path = ) local copy. NULL (default) disables HPO filtering; behaviour byte-identical omitting argument. vc_nonSyn Logical character vector. Controls Variant_Classification values retained (mirroring convention vc_nonSyn argument). FALSE (default) keeps variant classifications. TRUE keeps protein-altering classes (High/Moderate VEP consequences): "Frame_Shift_Del", "Frame_Shift_Ins", "Splice_Site", "Translation_Start_Site", "Nonsense_Mutation", "Nonstop_Mutation", "In_Frame_Del", "In_Frame_Ins", "Missense_Mutation". Alternatively, pass custom character vector classifications keep. Rows missing/blank Variant_Classification always removed filter active. canonical_only Logical; accepted API symmetry read.gvr(). SnpEff's ANN field CANONICAL flag, filter applied. TRUE (default), read.gvr.snpeff() emits one-time warning returns unfiltered table (result canonical_only = FALSE). ncores Integer; number worker processes converting MULTIPLE input files parallel via parallel::mclapply() (fork-based; Unix/macOS ). Default 1L runs sequentially byte-identical previous behaviour. Values > 1 help one VCF read (file independent task) clamped min(ncores, detectCores(), n_files). non-fork platforms falls back sequential. single file unaffected. normalize_alleles Logical; TRUE (default, since 0.99.2) apply bcftools-norm-style trimming common REF/ALT prefix suffix nucleotides deriving MAF-like coords. See read.gvr() full rationale. Set FALSE reproduce pre-0.99.2 coords reproducibility older analysis; recommended new research. verbose Logical; TRUE (default) print per-file per-chunk progress (file /N, cumulative records, elapsed seconds).

title: Convert SnpEff-annotated germline VCF(s) to an MAF-like data.table — read.gvr.snpeff
text: MAF-like data.table: one row per variant allele, MAF-like core columns, canonical 80 VEP CSQ field names (populated SnpEff ANN available; blank otherwise), key GATK QC fields, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, (enabled) Genotype ABraOM_AF columns. attr(., "annotator") = "snpeff". TSV/RDS/XLSX files written side effect write_tsv/write_rds/write_xlsx TRUE.

title: Convert SnpEff-annotated germline VCF(s) to an MAF-like data.table — read.gvr.snpeff
text: Output behaviour: Returns final MAF-like data.table, one row per variant ALLELE (multi-allelic sites split). Multiple ANN annotation blocks per allele reduced one -severe transcript per allele. Columns include MAF-like core fields, canonical 80 VEP CSQ field names (populated equivalent SnpEff ANN fields available; blank fields SnpEff supply), key GATK QC fields. FILTER retained column variants (PASS non-PASS) kept. Tumor_Seq_Allele1/Tumor_Seq_Allele2 zygosity-aware (vcf2maf- style), optional Genotype column (Tumor_Seq_Allele1/Tumor_Seq_Allele2, e.g. "T/C") added next alleles. variant keeps source sample Tumor_Sample_Barcode. Absent values written empty string "" (NA); downstream gvr_filter() / gvr_summary() treat NA "" identically "missing". Output tagged attr(gvr, "annotator") = "snpeff" downstream code can distinguish source. SnpEff-specific notes: nested .detect_annotator() helper inspects ##INFO=<ID=* header lines returns "snpeff" ##INFO=<ID=ANN found ("vep" ##INFO=<ID=CSQ found). present, VEP takes priority. nested get_ann_fields() helper parses SnpEff's ANN INFO header line, extracting 'F1 | F2 | ... | FN' field-name vector inside single-quoted Description= block. nested .snpeff_strip_allele() helper handles SnpEff's two non-standard allele forms (Cingolani spec): cancer-somatic-vs-germline ("G-C" -> "G") compound ("C-chr1:123456_A>T" -> "C"). Standard ALT alleles (dash) pass unchanged. Population AF columns (gnomADe_AF, etc.) populated first matching SnpEff ANN-side INFO key .AF_FIELDS_SNPEFF (case-sensitive, first match wins). Bare AF intentionally excluded - VCF spec per-ALT caller frequency, population frequency. Processing options (read.gvr()): MULTI-FILE: folder mode, every file matching pattern (default "*_NN.vcf.gz") converted row-bound. HGVS CLEANUP (strip_hgvs_prefix): strips Ensembl feature prefix HGVSc/HGVSp (e.g. "ENST00000831140.1:n.1889G>" -> "n.1889G>"). DEDUP (dedup_columns): removes duplicate-named columns values byte--byte identical across rows (otherwise keeps + warns). ABraOM (add_abraom): joins Brazilian ABraOM SABE-609 allele frequency ABraOM_AF column (downloaded/cached abraom_url). GENOTYPE-QUALITY FILTER (min_DP/min_GQ): keeps record iff DP > min_DP GQ > min_GQ; mirrors bcftools view -e 'FORMAT/DP<=X | FORMAT/GQ<=Y'. Set either NULL disable field; set NULL disable genotype filter entirely. GENE SUBSET (genes): restrict set Hugo_Symbols (exact, case-insensitive).

title: Convert SnpEff-annotated germline VCF(s) to an MAF-like data.table — read.gvr.snpeff
code: ## The function signature is exported and callable: is.function(read.gvr.snpeff) #> [1] TRUE  # \donttest{ ## read.gvr.snpeff() expects VCFs annotated by SnpEff (ANN/LOF/NMD ## INFO fields). The shipped example VCF is VEP-annotated, so a real ## SnpEff example needs your own VCFs; we therefore guard each call on ## the path existing, so the example skips cleanly on machines without ## the data. snpeff_dir <- "/path/to/snpeff-vcfs/" if (dir.exists(snpeff_dir)) {     gvr <- read.gvr.snpeff(snpeff_dir)      ## Or via the auto-router when the VCF header declares SnpEff fields:     gvr <- read.gvr(snpeff_dir) }  ## Single-file mode: full path snpeff_vcf <- "/path/to/SAMPLE_01.snpeff.vcf.gz" if (file.exists(snpeff_vcf)) {     gvr <- read.gvr.snpeff(vcf_path = snpeff_vcf) } # }

title: germlinevaR 0.99.2
text: read.gvr.dual() longer fails VCFs whose multi-ALT indel records share left-anchored REF base. Previously, reader built SnpEff side-table keyed (chrom, pos, ref, alt) using untrimmed coordinates, collapsed real distinct ALT alleles onto key threw duplicate-key error join step. reader now trims common left-anchor right-anchor bases (ref, alt) pair computing MAF-style coordinates (see ), ALT allele gets unique key matches canonical form used VEP side.

title: germlinevaR 0.99.2
text: Coordinates emitted read.gvr(), read.gvr.snpeff(), read.gvr.dual() indel ALT alleles now MAF-normalised via bcftools norm-equivalent trim conversion. Downstream tables (Start_Position, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2) reflect trimmed representation. SNVs unaffected. Example (chr1:6095864, TCCCCCCCCCTGCCC, TCCCCCCCCCCTGCCC,TCCCCACCCCCTGCCC): pre-0.99.2 behaviour available via normalize_alleles = FALSE every reader, purely escape hatch reproducing historical outputs; re-exposes multi-ALT collision bug recommended new analyses.

title: germlinevaR 0.99.2
text: New normalize_alleles argument (default TRUE) read.gvr(), read.gvr.snpeff(), read.gvr.dual(). Controls whether ALT-allele coordinates MAF-normalised via bcftools norm-style left--right trimming conversion. Setting FALSE restores pre-0.99.2 output byte-level comparison legacy tables. read.gvr.dual() output now carries snpeff_collisions_discarded attribute (via attr()) SnpEff side-table residual (chrom, pos, ref, alt) collisions MAF-normalisation. attribute data.table rows dropped Annotation_Impact-ranked deduper, downstream code can audit SnpEff annotations superseded. Absent collisions occur. Residual collisions expected homopolymer runs two ALT alleles collapse MAF-key even trimming (e.g. chr10:68173832 accompanying test fixture). Verbose mode read.gvr.dual() prints new one-line summary Annotation_Impact-ranked dedupe pass SnpEff side-table residual collisions collapsed (format: snpeff collisions: N MAF-key tuple(s) M duplicate row(s); collapsed Annotation_Impact rank).

title: germlinevaR 0.99.2
text: New unexported helper .gvr_trim_alleles() implements bcftools-norm left--right trim used .gvr_coords(). New unexported helper .gvr_dual_dedupe_snpeff() implements Annotation_Impact-ranked collision-resolution pass SnpEff side-table, gated behind normalize_alleles path. SnpEff-reader’s inlined gvr_coords() closure now delegates shared .gvr_coords() consistency across three readers.

title: germlinevaR 0.99.1
text: New gvr_hpo_genes() helper: resolves one Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO) term id(s) annotated gene vectors using HPO phenotype_to_genes.txt file. Lenient input forms accepted normalised ("HP:0003002", "hp:0003002", "hp:3002", "3002", "0003002" resolve canonical "HP:0003002"). Downloads caches annotation per user tools::R_user_dir("germlinevaR", "cache"); cache auto-refreshed 30 days. Descendants expanded (exact-term match ). offline / hermetic runs, point options(gvr.hpo_path = ) local copy file. New hpo argument read.gvr(), read.gvr.snpeff(), read.gvr.dual(), gvr_filter(). accepts one HPO term id(s); resolved genes UNION-ed existing genes panel vectors downstream filtering machinery runs. Passing hpo = NULL (default) byte-identical omitting argument. Verbose mode prints post-filter hpo subset: coverage line showing HPO-derived genes survived filtering, alongside existing panel subset: line. gvr_filter() gains panel argument API symmetry readers. Previously read.gvr() / read.gvr.snpeff() / read.gvr.dual() accepted panel. gvr_filter() now unions genes, panel, hpo identically, pre-loaded table can sliced disease panel phenotype without going readers .

title: germlinevaR 0.99.0
text: Initial Bioconductor submission. read.gvr(): auto-routing entry point VEP-, SnpEff-, dual-annotated germline VCFs. Inspects VCF header delegates appropriate sibling reader automatically. read.gvr.snpeff(): dedicated reader SnpEff-annotated VCFs (INFO ANN field). read.gvr.dual(): reader VCFs carrying VEP CSQ SnpEff ANN annotations records. gvr_filter(): modular filtering population allele frequency (gnomAD exomes, 1000 Genomes, ABraOM), ClinVar clinical significance, biotype, variant classification, genotype quality, gene panel. gvr_novel(): dedicated subsetter candidate novel variants (dbSNP rsID allele frequency catalogue). gvr_summary(): 8-section cohort summary returning list data.tables, optional Excel workbook, multi-page PDF, self-contained interactive HTML dashboard (plotly + DT). gvr_plot(): ComplexHeatmap-based top-genes variant matrix across samples, coloured variant classification. gvr_lollipop(): per-gene protein-domain lollipop plot --fly InterPro domain fetching (disk-cached) 52-palette colour system. gvr_genepos.plot(): per-gene gene-structure (cDNA) lollipop plot exon/intron/UTR regions resolved via Ensembl REST local GTF file (disk-cached). gvr_sum_plots(): standalone PNG/SVG/PDF exports dashboard panels produced gvr_summary(). gvr_color_palette(), gvr_list_palettes(): unified 52-palette colour system colourblind-safe defaults shared across every figure. gvr_panel_genes(), gvr_list_panels(): curated disease gene-panel registry (breast cancer, Lynch syndrome, GIST, others). gvr_domain_cache_clear(): cache management InterPro domain data.
